message:
[high] discord: validate api_url/gateway_url components + close PoolExhausted match

Two issues in the discord connector's URL-config surface:

1. validate_network_constraints_hosts() only checked the host portion
   of api_url / gateway_url (via host_allowed_by_network_constraints).
   Query string, fragment, userinfo, and scheme were all unvalidated,
   and DiscordApiClient builds every request URL via
       format!("{}{}", self.base_url, endpoint)
   (see connectors/discord/src/api.rs:136 and :184). A configure
   payload like {"api_url": "https://discord.com/api/v10?leak=x"}
   would pass the host allowlist and then leak `?leak=x` on every
   downstream request with the endpoint path tacked onto the query.
   Plain http on a public host would also slip through.

2. connectors/discord/src/error.rs had a non-exhaustive match on
   HttpClientError (= asupersync::ClientError) that stopped compiling
   once asupersync added the PoolExhausted { host, port } variant
   upstream. fcp-discord would not build from a clean checkout
   against the current asupersync main.

Patches:

- New validate_discord_endpoint_url(raw, field) enforces non-empty,
  parseable URL, scheme in {https, http, wss, ws}, plain http/ws
  only on localhost/127.0.0.1/::1, no userinfo, no query, no
  fragment. Called from validate_network_constraints_hosts() before
  the existing host allowlist check so the typed rejection fires
  first and host-based rejection stays as the second layer.
- Added a PoolExhausted arm to the HttpClientError → DiscordHttpErrorInfo
  conversion, tagging the failure as `is_connect=true` (pool pressure
  is a connection-layer problem, not a timeout, and the remote
  response has no status code).
- Eight new unit tests on the validator: accept (discord.com https,
  discord.gg wss, localhost http, 127.0.0.1 ws), reject (query,
  fragment, userinfo, bad scheme, empty, plain-http-on-public-host).

Verification: `cargo check -p fcp-discord` under
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-p7 is clean. `cargo test -p
fcp-discord --lib` cannot link right now because fcp-testkit
transitively depends on fcp-mesh, and uncommitted parallel-agent
WIP in crates/fcp-mesh/src/{node,symbol_request}.rs leaves a
SymbolRequestError::UnauthorizedZone variant uncovered in an
existing match — that breakage is in another pane's domain and
outside this commit's scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
